perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Proprietary/Confidential Information" shall mean any and all proprietary, secret information, technical data or know-how related to any aspect of other party's business or technology including, without limitation, data, know- how, formulas, designs, photographs, drawings, specification, software programs and samples and any other material bearing or incorporating any such information which is disclosed by one party to the other, which information, data or know-how is marked or stipulated as being "Proprietary", "Confidential", "Strictly Private" or otherwise, using words of similar significance. Such disclosure may be made either directly or indirectly, in writing, orally or by drawings, plans or inspection of products, materials, parts or equipment.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Proprietary/Confidential Information" shall mean information one party deems confidential.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly narrows the definition of 'Proprietary/Confidential Information', removing the detailed list of what it includes, such as 'technical data', 'formulas', 'designs', and the requirement for specific markings ('Proprietary', 'Confidential', etc.). This omission creates an in-text contradiction because later clauses rely on the original, more expansive definition. Specifically, Section 5 mentions 'such Proprietary/Confidential Information' but the new definition provides insufficient clarity on what constitutes Proprietary/Confidential Information to fall under this section, particularly orally disclosed information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event the disclosing party of such Proprietary/Confidential Information orally discloses the information to the receiving party, the disclosing party agrees to promptly notify the receiving party of the confidentiality of such oral disclosure and reduces to writing such Proprietary/Confidential Information and submit the same to the receiving party within 15 days of such oral disclosure, upon which the receiving party shall not be bound by the confidentiality obligations as herein provided as regards the said Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed orally.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Proprietary/Confidential Information', removing specific examples and the requirement for markings. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes a broader definition and specifies actions to be taken when such information is disclosed orally. The new, vague definition makes it unclear what oral disclosures would be covered, creating a direct conflict and rendering the contradicted section difficult to enforce. The original text defines the information to include technical data, formulas, etc. The change removes all of that and makes it any information one party deems confidential. The contradictory section requires the disclosing party to 'promptly notify the receiving party of the confidentiality of such oral disclosure and reduces to writing such Proprietary/Confidential Information', but the new definition does not specify if information has to be technical data, formulas etc. and any confidential information is only what one party deems it. So the receiving party may think the information disclosed is not what the disclosing party 'deems confidential' which breaks the compatibility of the changed text and contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of 5 years from the date of this Agreement, the receiving party shall not publish, disseminate nor disclose any Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties to any third party accept to those of its own employment having valid need to know the information in the course of employment and such disclosure shall be on terms not less restrictive than those herein contained.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall not disclose any Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties to any third party except to those of its own employment having valid need to know the information in the course of employment and such disclosure shall be on terms not less restrictive than those herein contained.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "For a period of 5 years from the date of this Agreement", the duration of the confidentiality obligation is eliminated. This creates an internal contradiction, as Section 7, 'SURVIVAL The aforesaid obligations of the receiving party shall survive the termination of this Agreement.' becomes unclear. Survival for how long is undefined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The aforesaid obligations of the receiving party shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 5-year confidentiality period. The changed text removes this duration. The contradicted text states the confidentiality obligations survive termination of the agreement. Without a defined period in the changed text, it is unclear how long the obligations survive, creating a contradiction because the survival term has no defined endpoint anymore. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Proprietary/Confidential Information as it uses in respect of its own information of like importance but in no case less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall use a degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Proprietary/Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "as it uses in respect of its own information of like importance but in no case less than a reasonable degree of care.", the standard of care is no longer defined. Section 9 states: 'REMEDY FOR BREACH It is understood and agreed between the parties that any breach of the obligations of confidentiality contained in this Agreement may cause the disclosing party irreparable loss.'. It will become extremely difficult to assess if the breach was a result of improper degree of care.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood and agreed between the parties that any breach of the obligations of confidentiality contained in this Agreement may cause the disclosing party irreparable loss. Accordingly, and in addition to any other remedies a party may have in law or equity, the disclosing party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the receiving party to prevent any further or continuing breach of the receiving party's obligations or additional damage to the disclosing party in the event such loss is in fact incurred by the disclosing party as a result of the breach or is imminent.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined a standard of care using an objective measure ('same degree of care... but in no case less than a reasonable degree of care'). The changed text removes this objective measure, leaving the standard of care undefined. Section 9, the contradicted text, discusses remedies for breach of confidentiality. The perturbation explanation notes that without a defined standard of care, it becomes extremely difficult to assess if a breach was a result of improper degree of care. This makes it difficult to enforce the 'obligations of confidentiality' mentioned in Section 9, thus creating a strong contradiction between the changed text and Section 9. The change creates ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects a standard to measure any breach.

perturbation.original_text: Is furnished to a third party by a party hereunto who owns such Proprietary/Confidential Information without similar restriction on the third party's rights;
perturbation.changed_text: Is furnished to a third party by a party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "hereunto who owns such Proprietary/Confidential Information without similar restriction on the third party's rights;", it implies that a party can furnish confidential information to a third party without any restriction on their right, in the absence of any obligation. This is against the definition of Proprietary/Confidential information under the mutual agreement and creates a contradiction within the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereby undertakes to treat and maintain all Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties in confidence. With respect thereto, each party hereby undertakes and agrees as follows: <PAGE> i. For a period of 5 years from the date of this Agreement, the receiving party shall not publish, disseminate nor disclose any Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties to any third party accept to those of its own employment having valid need to know the information in the course of employment and such disclosure shall be on terms not less restrictive than those herein contained. ii. The receiving party shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Proprietary/Confidential Information as it uses in respect of its own information of like importance but in no case less than a reasonable degree of care. iii. The receiving party shall in accordance with the request of the other parties, either return all copies, recording and tangible manifestations of Proprietary/Confidential Information or destroy the same following a determination by any of the parties not to enter into any arrangement or venture with each other of the kind contemplated herein or upon termination of any related memorandum of understanding or agreement entered into between the parties or upon the written request of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that if a party shares Proprietary/Confidential Information with a third party, there must be a similar restriction on the third party's rights. The changed text removes this restriction, implying that a party can furnish confidential information to a third party without any such restrictions. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which mandates that each party must treat and maintain all Proprietary/Confidential Information received from other parties in confidence, with restrictions no less restrictive than those contained in the agreement, thereby creating an in-text contradiction as defined in the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: All Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or confirming any rights by license or otherwise expressly impliedly or otherwise, for any of the Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed by the disclosing party hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: All information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "Proprietary/Confidential", this section now refers to 'all information'. However, the agreement is specifically about Proprietary/Confidential information. Section 3, 'EXCEPTIONS The aforesaid restrictions on the parties shall not apply to any Proprietary/Confidential Information which...' now become contradictory to the general definition provided here about 'all information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The aforesaid restrictions on the parties shall not apply to any Proprietary/Confidential Information which i. Can be proved by documentary evidence to be such Proprietary/Confidential Information that was already in the possession of the receiving party and at its free disposal before the disclosure hereunder to it; ii. Is received by the receiving party from third parties without accompanying secrecy or confidentiality obligations and not in violation of any duty of confidence under this agreement; iii. Is independently developed by the receiving party; iv. Is or becomes generally available to the public in printed publications in general discussion through no act or default on the part of the receiving party or its agents or employees; v. Is furnished to a third party by a party hereunto who owns such Proprietary/Confidential Information without similar restriction on the third party's rights; vi. Is approved for release by written authorization of the other party; or vii. Is disclosed pursuant to any requirement or request by operation of law provided that the involving party shall prior to disclosure notify the disclosing party of any such requirement or request.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers specifically to 'Proprietary/Confidential Information'. The changed text broadens this to 'all information'. Section 3 defines exceptions specifically for 'Proprietary/Confidential Information'. By removing the 'Proprietary/Confidential' qualifier in the changed text, it creates a contradiction because Section 3's exceptions no longer clearly apply only to proprietary or confidential information. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document since the general definition of information is contradicted by specific definitions outlined later.

perturbation.original_text: The disclosing party shall obtain the required authorizations, permits and licenses.
perturbation.explanation: By completely removing the sentence "The disclosing party shall obtain the required authorizations, permits and licenses." from Section 6, it now places the obligation and responsibility for obtaining required permits and licenses in an uncertain state. This removal creates a contradiction in enforcement and liability, especially considering the previous sentences state the licenses 'will be obtained prior to the exportation/disclosure' and 'The disclosing party shall notify the receiving party of the need'. Without a clear obligation on either party, any violation of export laws becomes difficult to assign responsibility to, making the prior statements about needing authorizations/permits and the disclosing party needing to notify the receiving party, effectively unenforceable in some situations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that necessary authorizations, permits or licenses including expert licenses as may be required will be obtained prior to the exportation/disclosure of any Proprietary/Confidential Information relating to the technology of the other party. The disclosing party shall notify the receiving party of the need to obtain any required authorizations, permits and licenses and/or the need to comply with any relevant laws or regulations relating to the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text placed an obligation on the disclosing party to obtain permits and licenses. The changed text removes this obligation entirely. This removal contradicts the section which states that 'necessary authorizations, permits or licenses... will be obtained prior to the exportation/disclosure' and that 'The disclosing party shall notify the receiving party of the need to obtain any required authorizations'. Removing the obligation creates a contradiction, as it is no longer clear who is responsible for obtaining the permits and licenses mentioned in the contradicted text, making enforcement and assigning liability uncertain, as explained in the perturbation explanation.
